content.text: Next feedback item: On entiredb (Go, ~2,600 files), I ran: entire graph search --query "how does quorum replication work". The top results were dominated by test workloads (PushQuorumWorkload.Check, QuorumInvariantWorkload.Check) and a docs ADR heading. The actual implementation — checkQuorum in objstore/packfile_fanout.go — did not appear in the top 6; it showed up only indirectly as the QuorumError type at #8 and a CLAUDE.md pointer at #20. The result looked valid but pointed at tests instead of the implementation. The stats reported graph_candidates: 0, so ranking used lexical/BM25 matching only. What makes this notable: the edges command DOES correctly know checkQuorum's real callers (DissStore.replicateObject, repoScopedStorer.StorePackfileQuarantine, with file:line evidence) — so the graph already has the signal needed to rank the implementation first; search just isn't using it.

I'll investigate this feedback item: graph search returned test workloads over the real implementation, and `graph_candidates: 0` suggests the graph signal never entered ranking. Let me explore how search builds its candidates.

1	# Agent Instructions
2	
3	This repository is **Entire Graph** (`entire-graph`), an Entire CLI plugin that
4	provides entity-level checkpoint context and a local-only semantic provider.
5	The binary is invoked through Entire as `entire graph <command>`.
6	
7	## Naming
8	
9	- Product name: **Entire Graph**. Binary and module name: `entire-graph`
10	  (module `github.com/entireio/entire-graph`).
11	- The plugin manifest (`entire-plugin.yml`) registers the subcommand name
12	  `graph`, so users type `entire graph ...`.
13	- The project was previously named `entire-sem`; do not reintroduce the old
14	  name. References to **Entire Brain** (`entire-brain`) are intentional — that
15	  is the separate downstream consumer that owns persistence, indexing, and
16	  query, not an old name for this project.
17	
18	## Build, Test, Lint
19	
20	Tasks are defined in `mise.toml`:
21	
22	```sh
23	mise run build     # go build -o entire-graph ./cmd/entire-graph
24	mise run test      # go test ./...
25	mise run test:ci   # go test -race ./...
26	mise run fmt       # gofmt -s -w .
27	mise run vet       # go vet ./...
28	mise run check     # fmt + vet + race tests + build
29	```
30	
31	CI (`.github/workflows/test.yml`) runs vet, gofmt checks, build, and tests on
32	Linux, macOS, and Windows with Go 1.24. Building requires CGO because
33	tree-sitter grammars are native.
34	
35	## Using the plugin from an agent session
36	
37	- `entire graph search --repo . --query "..."` — ranked source regions with
38	  bounded snippets (16 KiB serialized context budget by default). Reads the
39	  working tree by default so dirty edits are visible; `--head` switches to
40	  committed-tree semantics.
41	- `entire graph commit HEAD --json` / `entire graph diff --base A --head B --json` —
42	  entity-level semantic diff for judging whether a change is safe to keep.
43	- `entire graph checkpoint <id> --json` — resolves the commit carrying an
44	  `Entire-Checkpoint: <id>` trailer and diffs it against its first parent.
45	- `entire graph snapshot|symbols|edges --repo . --format ndjson` — streamed
46	  machine-readable graph records (schema 1.x, frozen GA contract; see
47	  `docs/adr/0001-ga-schema-contract.md`).
48	- `entire graph capabilities --json` / `entire graph doctor --json` — feature
49	  detection and environment verification. All commands are local-only:
50	  no network egress, no hosted model calls, no telemetry.
51	
52	This repository also configures agent harnesses directly: `.claude/settings.json`
53	and `.codex/` wire Entire session hooks into Claude Code and Codex, and both
54	define an `entire-search` subagent for checkpoint history search via
55	`entire search --json`.
56	
57	## Repository layout
58	
59	- `cmd/entire-graph/` — thin main; all logic lives in `internal/`.
60	- `internal/cli/` — hand-rolled command dispatch and flag parsing (no CLI
61	  framework by design). Keep new commands consistent with this style.
62	- `internal/sem/` — tree-sitter parsing, provider snapshots, semantic diff,
63	  and hybrid search. This is the only package that should know about grammars.
64	- `internal/gitutil/` — git subprocess wrappers (no go-git; NUL-delimited
65	  output parsing throughout).
66	- `internal/bench/`, `cmd/graph-bench/`, `bench/` — pinned-corpus benchmark
67	  harness with throughput and RSS guardrails.
68	- `docs/` — provider requirements, v2 roadmap, language support matrix,
69	  benchmarks, and the GA schema ADR.
70	
71	## Contract rules that agents must not break
72	
73	- Schema `1.x` is a frozen GA contract: minor versions are additive-only;
74	  breaking wire-format changes require a major bump (`docs/adr/0001-ga-schema-contract.md`).
75	- The provider is no-egress: never add code that fetches remote sources,
76	  calls hosted APIs, uploads telemetry, or downloads grammars at runtime.
77	- `compound-v1` symbol IDs must remain stable across ordinary body and
78	  signature edits.
79	- Unsupported or unparseable files must surface as machine-readable partial
80	  failures, never silent drops.
81	- Inventory-only languages must not be presented as semantically parsed
82	  (`docs/language-support.md`).

input.command:
git add -A && git status --short && git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(search): use call-graph signal to rank implementations above test scaffolding

On the entiredb corpus (~2,600 Go files), "how does quorum replication
work" ranked test workloads (PushQuorumWorkload.Check) and a docs ADR
heading above the actual implementation (checkQuorum in
objstore/packfile_fanout.go), which did not appear in the top 6 even
though the graph knew its production callers. Stats showed
graph_candidates: 0 — ranking was purely lexical. Four compounding
defects:

1. Search defaulted to the syntax-only profile, which emits no CALLS
   edges at all, so graph expansion could never fire on the default path.
2. The fast profile's shallow call resolution kept only same-file
   ("exact") calls and never built the cross-file symbol index, dropping
   the same-package edges (confidence 0.80) that connect an
   implementation to its callers.
3. Graph-expansion candidates were scored 0.28*seed+confidence (~5
   points against lexical scores of 13-17), making expansion decorative
   even at the full profile.
4. No ranking signal distinguished a symbol with real production
   callers from same-named test scaffolding.

Fixes:

- Search now defaults to the fast profile (CLI and library). Marginal
  uncached index cost is ~0.6-1.1s on 2,600-3,000-file corpora; the
  tree-keyed cache absorbs it for committed-tree runs.
- Shallow call resolution retains all single-target high-precision
  resolutions (exact, package, import_resolved) and builds the global
  short-name index it needs to find cross-file targets; name-only and
  pattern fanouts remain full-only.
- New caller-degree boost: min(8, 2.6*log2(1+distinct production
  callers)) with a "graph:callers" signal and a
  caller_boosted_candidates stat. Callers under test paths do not count,
  and body-only matches receive half boost so well-connected symbols
  that merely mention a query word cannot displace name/path evidence.
- Graph-expansion scores inherit 0.55*seed (plus edge confidence, path
  prior, and the target's caller boost) when the neighbor carries at
  least one query term, keeping the conservative 0.28*seed for
  zero-evidence neighbors so utility helpers cannot displace direct
  matches.
- Test-artifact path penalty raised from -1.5 to -4 (still query-aware:
  lifted entirely when the query mentions tests/specs/fixtures).

On entiredb, "how does quorum replication work" now returns checkQuorum
first with its production caller DissStore.replicateObject second; the
test workloads follow. Test-intent queries ("how do the simulation
tests assert quorum invariants") still rank the tests first.
